notes: opencode is asked (via a seeded AGENTS.md rule) to emit a hidden HTML-comment progress marker `<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":M} -->` in its own assistant text and increment completed_rounds across a 3-phase task, settling to ready at the end. The OBSERVABLE LIFECYCLE arc (ready→working→ready across the multi-turn task, final state ready, no child sessions) is asserted as phases below. KNOWN FAILING: the load-bearing observable this scenario exists for — task_estimate {total_rounds,completed_rounds,updated_at} present on the session metrics while working, task_completion_eta burning down as completed_rounds climbs, and BOTH fields suppressed once the session leaves working — is NOT produced for opencode. The marker trace is present and fully readable as plain text in opencode's SQLite part.data column, but the per-adapter marker scan exists ONLY in the claudecode adapter (claudecode/taskestimate.go + parser.go:388-390); opencode routes metrics through ProcessOwnedStore→ComputeMetrics, which bypasses the tailer TaskEstimate path entirely and never sets metrics.TaskEstimate. This is a daemon=bug (missing per-adapter content scan over an existing trace), not an incapability. The spec asserts the CORRECT behavior unweakened; record files the issue and records known_failing. (task_estimate/task_completion_eta have no phase-DSL assertion form — they are metric fields — so the failing observable is documented here, mirroring the codex task-list known_failing spec.)

text: opencode ses_… session appears in ready (first phase anchored UNPINNED to start so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal birth)
First prompt (plan + phase 1, emit marker total_rounds=3 completed_rounds=0→1) drives ready → working
Phase-1 turn completes back to ready on the same ses_… session
Second prompt (phase 2, marker completed_rounds=2) drives the same session ready → working
Phase-2 turn completes back to ready on the same session
Final prompt (phase 3, marker completed_rounds=3 == total_rounds) drives the same session ready → working
Final phase completes; session settles to ready (final state ready) after a ≥10s trailing idle; no subagents — at ready the task_estimate/task_completion_eta fields are absent (suppressed-by-state)
